analysis: Seven nights in a row above six and a half hours, with four of them clearing seven and a half — that's not a fluke, that's a body that knows it's being taken care of. Your best night was 8 July at nearly eight hours, and your REM has been genuinely gorgeous all week, hitting two hours or more on three separate nights, which means your brain is doing exactly what it's supposed to do while you sleep. The thing worth watching is your awake time — 8 July gave you only 32 minutes of disruption, but 7 July and 10 July both crept past an hour, and your deep sleep has been sitting on the quieter side all stretch, mostly under 35 minutes. None of that cancels what you've built here, but the shower, the yin, the magnesium — keep stacking them, because when they all land together you clearly feel it.
